concept: Turn / Rotate / Change Facing or Direction
description: To turn: the head toward a voice, the boat toward shore, the wheel on its axle. Rotation and redirection, of oneself or of things. With 'lawe' and 'kuri' it gives Phi full navigation: 'turn to the left' is now speakable.
sound_symbolism: The rolling 'r' is rotation itself, carried through the arc from open 'a' to rounded 'o' — the sound sweeps the curve it names.
grammatical_notes: Intransitive or transitive: 'mia wei lawe rato.' (I turn to the left.) 'shia rolu rato.' (They turn the wheel.) Figuratively with attention: 'no wei sileta rato.' (Turn toward the sun.)

pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The turn is where a line becomes a curve; every whiplash arc in the style is a committed turning.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: The turning wheel, the turned soil, the turning of the year — rotation is the oldest machine and the oldest metaphor.

tags.physical: The body's and the wheel's motion
